person.name: Sir Geoffrey Boleyn II
person.alternateName: Sir Geoffrey Boleyn Lord Mayor of London

memoryArtifacts.headline: THE ANCESTRY OF SIR PAON DE RUET, FATHER-IN-LAW OF GEOFFREY CHAUCER AND OF JOHN ‘OF GAUNT’
Hever Castle
memoryArtifacts.description:
Geoffrey Boleyn was the son of Geoffrey Boleyn (d. 1440) yeoman of Salle, Norfolk, and Alice,[1] and grandson of Thomas Boleyn (d. 1411) of Salle and Anne, an heiress, daughter of Sir John Bracton, a Norfolk knight. He went to London, was apprenticed to a hatter, and became a freeman of the city through the Hatter’s Company in 1428. In 1429 he transferred to a grander livery company, the Mercers’ Company, of which he became master in 1454.[2] As a wealthy mercer he served as a Sheriff of London in 1447, as member of parliament for the city in 1449, as alderman in 1452, and Lord Mayor of London in 1457/8,[3] and was knighted[4] by King Henry VI.[5] He purchased the manor of Blickling in Norfolk from Sir John Fastolf in 1452, and Hever Castle in Kent in 1462.[5]
He was buried in the church of St Lawrence Jewry in the City of London.[5]

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Geoffrey Boleyn II於1406年出生時，他的父親Sir Geoffrey Boleyn I26歲，母親Lady Alice Bracton21歲。他於1425年與Anne Elizabeth Hoo在英國英格蘭諾福爾可薩勒結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他於1457年被列為alderman of bassishaw 。他去世於1463年7月2日享年57歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可諾維其諾維其 凱德拉爾。

nameMeanings.name: Boleyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Bullen  .
Flemish: derivative of the personal name Bol, from Baldwin .

variants.name: Bullen
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from the French Channel port of Boulogne, recorded in Latin sources both as Gessoriacum and as Bononia. The latter name is clearly the source of the modern placename. It is ostensibly a derivative of Latin bonus ‘good’ (compare Bolognese ), but may in fact come from a Gaulish element bona ‘foundation’. Boulogne has long been a major trading port between England and France.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Geoffrey Boleyn II was born in 1406, his father, Sir Geoffrey Boleyn I, was 26 and his mother, Lady Alice Bracton, was 21. He married Anne Elizabeth Hoo in 1425, in Salle, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 6 daughters. In 1457, his occupation is listed as alderman of bassishaw . He died on 2 July 1463, at the age of 57, and was buried in Norwich Cathedral, Norwich, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

Il est décédé le 2 juillet 1463, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à Norwich Cathedral, Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Geoffrey Boleyn II(이)가 1406년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Geoffrey Boleyn I(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Alice Bracton(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1425년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 샐일에서 Anne Elizabeth Hoo(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1457년, 그의 직업은 alderman of bassishaw 이었다. 그는 1463년 7월 2일 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 노위치 노위치 캐테드럴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Geoffrey Boleyn II nasceu em 1406, seu pai, Sir Geoffrey Boleyn I, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Lady Alice Bracton, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Anne Elizabeth Hoo em 1425, em Salle, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 6 filhas. Em 1457, sua profissão aparece como alderman of bassishaw . Ele faleceu em 2 de julho de 1463, com 57 anos, e foi sepultado em Norwich Cathedral, Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Geoffrey Boleyn II родился в 1406 г., его отцу по имени Sir Geoffrey Boleyn I было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Alice Bracton, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Elizabeth Hoo в 1425 г. в местности салл, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 6 дочерей. В 1457 году он работал по профессии alderman of bassishaw . Он умер 2 июля 1463 г. в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности норвич кэтедраль, норвич, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.
